title: Minimum Changes to Make K Semi-palindromes
description:
Given a string s and an integer k, partition s into k substrings such that the letter changes needed to make each substring a semi-palindrome are minimized.
Return the minimum number of letter changes required.
A semi-palindrome is a special type of string that can be divided into palindromes based on a repeating pattern. To check if a string is a semi-palindrome:​
Consider the string "abcabc":
Example 1:
Example 2:
Example 3:
Constraints:
constraints: s contains only lowercase English letters.

examples.example_text:
Input: s = "abcac", k = 2
Output: 1
Explanation: Divide s into "ab" and "cac" . "cac" is already semi-palindrome. Change "ab" to "aa" , it becomes semi-palindrome with d = 1 .

Input: s = "abcdef", k = 2
Output: 2
Explanation: Divide s into substrings "abc" and "def" . Each needs one change to become semi-palindrome.

Input: s = "aabbaa", k = 3
Output: 0
Explanation: Divide s into substrings "aa" , "bb" and "aa" . All are already semi-palindromes.

hints: Define dp[i][j] as the minimum count of letter changes needed to split the suffix of string s starting from s[i] into j valid parts.
We have dp[i][j] = min(dp[x + 1][j - 1] + v[i][x]). Here v[i][x] is the minimum number of letter changes to change substring s[i..x] into semi-palindrome.
v[i][j] can be calculated separately by brute-force. We can create a table of v[i][j] independently to improve the complexity. Also note that semi-palindrome’s length is at least 2.
